vocab.meaning: nảy mầm, bắt đầu mọc lên nhanh chóng
vocab.example: I remember being so excited to see the tiny green shoots of my beans sprout up after just a few days.

vocab.meaning: cho cây vào chậu (thường là khi cây đã lớn hơn)
vocab.example: Once the seedlings were strong enough, my grandmother helped me pot them up into larger terracotta containers.

vocab.meaning: héo úa hoặc tàn đi (thường là tạm thời theo mùa)
vocab.example: The lilies I planted would die back in the winter, but they always returned more beautiful the following spring.

vocab.meaning: phát triển cực kỳ tốt nhờ vào một điều kiện nào đó
vocab.example: I tried growing cacti because they seemed to thrive on the intense sunlight on my bedroom windowsill.

vocab.meaning: chăm sóc (cây) khỏe lại sau khi bị héo hoặc bệnh
vocab.example: I found a withered aloe vera plant and spent weeks trying to nurse it back to health with careful watering.

vocab.meaning: mọc lan ra và chiếm lấy toàn bộ không gian
vocab.example: The mint I planted in the garden started to take over the entire flowerbed because it grew so aggressively.

vocab.meaning: tỉa bớt cành lá để cây phát triển tốt hơn
vocab.example: My father taught me how to cut back the rose bushes in autumn to encourage new growth for the next year.

vocab.meaning: khô héo hoàn toàn do thiếu nước
vocab.example: I once forgot to water my marigolds during a heatwave, and they completely dried out within a few days.

vocab.meaning: đào lên (thường là cây lấy củ hoặc để chuyển chỗ)
vocab.example: There was a sense of magic in digging up the potatoes I had planted and seeing how much they had grown underground.

vocab.meaning: làm lộ ra hoặc thúc đẩy (vẻ đẹp, màu sắc, tiềm năng)
vocab.example: The organic fertilizer we used really helped to bring out the vibrant colors of the hibiscus flowers.
